| v1.0     | new user  | see usage instructions    | refer to them when I forget how to use the application      |

1. Ensure that you have Java 17 or above installed.
2. Down the latest version of `InventoryBro` from [here](http://link.to/duke).
Adds a new item to the list of todo items.
* The `DEADLINE` can be in a natural language format.
`todo n/Write the rest of the User Guide d/next week`
`todo n/Refactor the User Guide to remove passive voice d/13/04/2020`
**Q**: How do I transfer my data to another computer?
* Main entry-point for the java.seedu.inventorybro.InventoryBro application.
System.out.println("How can I help you today, bro?");
* Represents an item with a description and quantity.
* Creates an item with the given description and quantity.
* Returns the item in save file format.
* @return A string formatted for writing to the save file.
* Returns the string of the item's description and quantity.
* @return The string format of the item's description and quantity.
* Manages the list of items, providing operations to add and delete items.
* Creates an item list pre-populated with the given items.
* @param items An existing list of items to manage.
* Adds a item to the list.
* Removes and returns the item at the given index.
* @param index Zero-based index of the item to delete.
* Returns the item at the given index.
* @param index Zero-based index of the item.
* Returns the number of items in the list.
* Returns true if there are no items in the list.
public static void parse(String line, ItemList items) {
System.out.println("Invalid command, please try add, delete, edit, transact, list, exit");
static void parseAdd(String text, ItemList items) {
* Adds a new item to the inventory.
private static final Pattern ADD_COMMAND_PATTERN = Pattern.compile("^addItem d/(.*?) q/(\\d+)$");
* Creates an add command from the raw user input.
* @param input The full add command string.
* Parses the add command input and appends the new item to the list.
* @param items The inventory item list to update.
"Invalid addItem format! Use: addItem d/NAME q/INITIAL_QUANTITY"
* Executes the command against the provided item list.
* @param items The inventory item list to operate on.
* Removes an item from the inventory using a one-based index.
* Creates a delete command from the raw user input.
* @param input The full delete command string.
* Parses the delete command input and removes the targeted item.
* @param items The inventory item list to update.
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid delete format! Use: deleteItem INDEX");
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid index! Please provide a valid item number.");
System.out.println("Noted, BRO. I've removed this item:");
System.out.println("Now you have " + items.size() + " items in the list.");
throw new IllegalArgumentException("The index must be a number! Use: deleteItem INDEX");
* Updates an existing item's description and quantity.
* Creates an edit command from the raw user input.
* @param input The full edit command string.
* Parses the edit command input and updates the targeted item.
* @param items The inventory item list to update.
System.out.println("Index and quantity must be numbers.");
* Prints the farewell message and exits the program.
* @param items The inventory item list, unused for this command.
* Creates a list command from the raw user input.
* @param input The full list command string.
* Validates the list command input and prints all items in the inventory.
* @param items The inventory item list to display.
System.out.println("Here are your current inventory items:");
* Adjusts an item's quantity by a signed transaction amount.
* Creates a transact command from the raw user input.
* @param input The full transact command string.
* Parses the transact command input and updates the targeted item's quantity.
* @param items The inventory item list to update.
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid index for transact.");
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Transaction failed. Quantity cannot go below 0.");
* Ensures that the provided string contains only digit characters.
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid transact, Index or Quantity Must be a digit");
* Ensures that the provided string is either a positive integer or a negative integer.
* @param digits The signed numeric string to validate.
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid transact. Quantity cannot be empty.");
throw new IllegalArgumentException("Invalid transact. Quantity cannot be just a minus sign.");
* Verifies that a valid add command creates a new item with the expected values.
* Verifies that an item description may contain multiple words.
* Verifies that malformed add input is rejected.
* Verifies that deleting a valid index removes the correct item.
* Verifies that out-of-range indices are rejected without mutating the list.
* Verifies that malformed delete input is rejected.
* Verifies that a valid edit command updates the targeted item.
* Verifies that editing works correctly for a later item in the list.
* Verifies that an out-of-bounds index is handled without throwing.
* Verifies that zero as an index is handled without throwing.
* Verifies that a non-numeric index is handled without throwing.
assertDoesNotThrow(() -> new EditCommand("edit abc d/Apple q/5").execute(items));
* Verifies that a non-numeric quantity is handled without throwing.
* Verifies that editing one item does not modify the others.
* Verifies that executing the exit command triggers application termination.
* Verifies that a valid negative quantity transaction decreases stock.
* Verifies that a valid positive quantity transaction increases stock.
* Verifies that a transaction may reduce the quantity exactly to zero.
* Verifies that transactions which would make the quantity negative are ignored.
* Verifies that invalid item indices do not change the quantity.
* Verifies that malformed transact inputs do not change the quantity.
* Verifies that a zero-quantity transaction leaves the quantity unchanged.
